title: Biserrula epiglottis
taxonomy: Biserrula epiglottis is a species of plant in the family Fabaceae. It is a legume native to Australia and New Guinea. Biserrula epiglottis is commonly known as the hairy biserrula. The species is classified as a shrub or a herb, growing up to 1 meter in height. Its classification is further broken down into the family Fabaceae, subfamily Faboideae, and tribe Loteae.

It is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help in treating various health conditions. intro: Biserrula epiglottis, also known as Biserrula, is a perennial legume native to the Mediterranean region, characterized by its distinctive shape and unique seed pods. The health benefits of Biserrula epiglottis include its potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce inflammation and protect against cell damage.
